author: tersec
content: wonder how Teku and Lodestar are managing to self-build on this: https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-6.ethpandaops.io/slots/filtered?f=&f.pname=geth&f.builderblock=0&f.status=0x7&f.pstatus=0x7&c=50&d=0x207ff

author: barnabasbusa
content: `2. teku-geth-1 self-builds and publishes the invalid gas limit anyway. Its own log says it plainly: Published block... Builder: 18446744073709551615, Bid gas limit: 189814455 (self-build) vs Builder: 2, Bid gas limit: 190000000 (buildoor bid). It runs no compatibility pre-check.`

author: barnabasbusa
content: teku one feels like a bug lol

author: barnabasbusa
content: ` And here's the kicker: those non-compliant blocks go canonical — slots 56966 and 56915 are Canonical with gas_limit: 189814455. The network does not enforce is_gas_limit_target_compatible in block processing; it only exists as (a) bid-gossip validation (nimbus Ignores such bids on gossip, which doesn't stop propagation) and (b) honest-validator self-build guidance. Nimbus is the only CL implementing (b), so nimbus-geth-1 misses proposals for being the only honest one, while teku-geth-1 ships spec-violating blocks and gets away with it `

author: tersec
content: that's a broken design

author: tersec
content: Nimbus should remove its check

author: tersec
content: i.e. broken protocol design

author: barnabasbusa
content: where does it say nimbus should remove the check

author: tersec
content: I don't care what Claude says

author: tersec
content: I mean, Nimbus loses by "honesty" here

author: tersec
content: because there's no enforcement

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah, no, nimbus is 100% right here

author: tersec
content: Right, but my point is broader

author: tersec
content: it's that this sets up incentive-misalignment

author: nflaig
content: I don't see what we do wrong, for local builds the CL does not check if the gas limit matches our target, that is a check on bid gossip topic

author: nflaig
content: if you submit invalid bids via lodestar api then it should not publish these invalid bids that do not respect the `target_gas_limit` of the proposer preferences

author: barnabasbusa
content: I think you should still implement the check to stay an "honest validator"

author: nflaig
content: what exactly is the problem?

author: barnabasbusa
content: you are submitting a block with invalid gas limit

author: barnabasbusa
content: according to `is_gas_limit_target_compatible` calculation

author: nflaig
content: how did those bids pass gossip validation?

author: barnabasbusa
content: local block

author: barnabasbusa
content: you didn't verify what geth gave you

author: nflaig
content: it's not relevant there

content: geth had a bug

author: nflaig
content: the block is not invalid

author: tersec
content: so this is sort of my point. this is technically true

author: tersec
content: why _not_ create a valid block indeed

author: nflaig
content: if the transition is valid per EIP-1559 it's fine, the target is whatever, it's not a validity concern

content: what exactly is the geth bug?

author: barnabasbusa
content: for targetGasLimit

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah

author: nflaig
content: nah no way we apply `is_gas_limit_target_compatible` on a local payload and reject it

author: nflaig
content: not a validity concern

author: barnabasbusa
content: can you at least log an error for it?

author: nflaig
content: but it seems to me the payload was valid, so that's better than rejecting it

author: barnabasbusa
content: even if you ignore it

author: nflaig
content: we do this for builder payload headers we receive today, and even reject them, but I am not sure it's the job of the CL to validate this for local payloads

author: barnabasbusa
content: I'd prefer if we had the same behaviour for local block building and builder block building tbh

author: nflaig
content: this is specified in the execution-apis spec and likely also the execution-specs

author: nflaig
content: generally we don't validate a lot in the local payload other than some structural checks

author: barnabasbusa
content: so you can't log it if you don't check it

author: nflaig
content: we don't even check if they set the correct fee recipient

author: barnabasbusa
content: ok in this case I'd argue that nimbus is allowed to check this but probably being a bit too strict ?

author: nflaig
content: so regarding this, yes if there are no bugs

author: barnabasbusa
content: real question is which is more important Liveness > correctness or liveness < correctness

author: nflaig
content: we can add the check and emit a warning, but I would not throw an error here

author: barnabasbusa
content: because lodestar and teku are liveness > correctness, and nimbus is correctness > liveness

author: barnabasbusa
content: I think you would need to check a lot more if you going down this path

author: nflaig
content: yes, generally we don't handhold the EL like that

author: nflaig
content: there is anyways plenty of stuff that is just opaque for the CL and needs to be correctly done by the EL

author: barnabasbusa
content: its also probably worth highlighting that having client diversity is probably the best here.

author: barnabasbusa
content: having some clients do A and some do B

author: nflaig
content: checking `is_gas_limit_target_compatible` on local payload is useful at devnet stage, afterwards it's not gonna do much, since Nimbus has it, we can use that to find ELs doing shady stuff

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah I wonder if we should have different rules for devnets and mainnet
